ScriptHelpSection.headerTitle: How to copy a YouTube transcript

sectionA.title: Getting a transcript from YouTube on desktop
sectionA.description: Follow these steps to copy the full transcript in about 1–2 minutes.

step1.title: Click the "···" or "More" button below the video.
step1.body: Look under the video title, next to the Like/Share buttons, for the three dots (···) or a "More" button.
step1.imageAlt: Location of the More (···) button on the YouTube watch page.

step2.title: In the menu, choose "Show transcript" or "Transcript".
step2.body: In the menu that opens, click the item named "Show transcript", "Captions", or "Transcript".
step2.imageAlt: YouTube menu showing the Show transcript option.

step3.title: Select the entire transcript on the right and copy it.
step3.tip1: 1. Click once on the first line of the transcript.
step3.tip2Part1: 2. Scroll all the way down, then
step3.tip2Part2: click the last line once to select the entire transcript.
step3.tip3: 3. Press Ctrl + C (or ⌘ + C on Mac) to copy.
step3.tipNote: ※ Using Ctrl + A selects the whole page, so we don’t recommend it when you only want the transcript.
step3.imageAlt: Selecting the entire transcript in the YouTube transcript panel.

step4.title: Paste it into the input box on the left of this page.
step4.body: Click the "Paste video script" input area, then press Ctrl + V (or ⌘ + V on Mac) to paste. Now you’re ready.
step4.imageAlt: Pasting text into the video script input field.

sectionB.title: Want to use subtitles or an editing tool instead?
sectionB.description: If you already have a project in progress, you can simply copy the text and paste it here.

case1.title: Copying subtitles or script from an editor (Premiere, CapCut, etc.)
case1.body: Select all subtitle text from the timeline, copy it, and paste it into the input on the left.

case2.title: Opening subtitle files (srt, vtt, etc.) as text
case2.body: Open the srt/vtt file in a text editor like Notepad. It will contain timestamps with dialogue. Copy the lines you need and paste them here.

case3.title: Using a script document you’ve already written
case3.body: It doesn’t matter where your script lives—blog, Word, Notion, etc. Just copy the full text and paste it as-is.

faq.title: Q. I don’t see a "Show transcript" / "Transcript" menu.
faq.item1: Some videos may hide the transcript at the creator’s choice.
faq.item2: There may be no subtitles at all, or it might be a live/real-time stream.
faq.item3: In that case, you’ll need to type it manually while listening, or use a separate subtitle generation tool.

mouse.description: When you're using a mouse, hold right click and drag to move around the map smoothly.
mouse.calloutDescription: Hold right click and drag the map.

trackpad.title: Use two fingers on a trackpad to move the map
trackpad.description: On a laptop trackpad, slide with two fingers to pan across the map naturally.
trackpad.calloutDescription: Use a two-finger gesture to move around.

context.title: Select a node, then right click
context.description: Select a node first, then right click to open a context menu with the available actions.
context.calloutDescription: The menu opens after selecting a node.

highlight.title: Use highlight to mark important parts
highlight.description: Click the middle icon to highlight a node. Mark important parts so they are much easier to revisit later.
highlight.calloutDescription: Highlight key parts while you read.

expand.title: Expand and collapse nodes to read more comfortably
expand.description: Click the + button inside the circle to reveal child content. You can collapse it again and follow only what you need, which makes the Structure Map much easier to read.
expand.calloutDescription: Click the + button to reveal child content.

terms.title: AI organizes difficult terms for you
terms.description: In the Terms tab on the left panel, AI can gather unfamiliar words from the content and explain them clearly.
terms.calloutDescription: AI finds difficult terms and organizes them for you.

shortcuts.title: It gets much easier once you remember the shortcuts
shortcuts.description: Once you remember the most useful shortcuts, working with a Structure Map becomes much faster. You can always open this again from More > Shortcuts.

edit.title: Editing is ready on mobile by default
edit.description: On mobile, the Structure Map opens in an editable state from the start.
edit.calloutDescription: You can start editing right away on mobile.

move.title: You can drag the map directly on mobile
move.description: On mobile, you do not need a separate move mode. Just drag with one finger on empty space or away from the node controls to pan the map.
move.calloutDescription: Try dragging with one finger to move the map.

select.title: Tap a node to select it, and long-press to open the edit menu
select.description: On mobile, a single tap selects a node. The small quick actions next to the node are available right away, and in Edit mode the bottom edit bar opens when you long-press.
select.calloutDescription: Tap a node to select it, then long-press when you want the edit menu.

highlight.title: Highlight important nodes as you read
highlight.description: When a node is selected, small quick-action buttons appear next to it. Use the highlight button there to mark important parts.
highlight.calloutDescription: Select a node, then tap the highlight button.

expand.title: Expand and collapse nodes to read more comfortably
expand.description: Tap the + button inside the circle to reveal child content. You can collapse it again and follow only what you need, which makes the Structure Map much easier to read.
expand.calloutDescription: Tap the + button to reveal child content.

panel.title: Open the left panel for more context
panel.description: Tap the left panel button to open the Info, Notes, and Terms tabs. While reading the Structure Map, you can quickly check whatever you need there.
panel.calloutDescription: This button opens the Info, Notes, and Terms tabs.

terms.title: AI organizes difficult terms for you
terms.description: In the Terms tab on the left panel, AI can gather unfamiliar words from the content and explain them clearly.
terms.calloutDescription: You can review difficult terms here.

CreatorSection.label: Creator
CreatorSection.name: Lina
CreatorSection.quote: “There’s always more to watch than there is time. I’d finish a video thinking it was incredibly helpful, but by the evening I could barely remember half of it. I kept telling myself I’d come back to it later, and of course many of those videos just disappeared into the backlog. With health, kids’ education, and personal finance all competing for attention, it became even easier to lose track. So I built a tool myself. When you save what you just watched as a “structure map”, you can reopen it later and still see the flow and the key points at a glance.”

MapLibrarySection.title: The more you compare and connect, the clearer your own standard becomes
MapLibrarySection.subtitle: Don’t stop at collecting scattered information. Compare it, organize it, and turn it into a standard you can actually rely on in your own judgment.

tags.title: Tag Organization
tags.description: Group Structure Maps by theme so it becomes easier to compare perspectives side by side.

merge.title: Merge Maps
merge.description: Connect multiple channels and sources into one flow so you can see the bigger picture.

edit.title: Edit Freely
edit.description: Refine key claims and reasoning in your own words as you shape your own standard.

read.title: Highlight and Notes
read.description: Return to the important parts and keep your focus even when different viewpoints conflict.

share.title: Share and Download
share.description: Keep the standards and insights you organized accessible as links or images.

one.quote: Organizing stock-related YouTube information into a Structure Map helped me grasp the key points quickly, compare multiple perspectives without being swayed in one direction, and make decisions based on my own standard.

two.quote: I kept wondering how I should raise my child, but I never had time to watch every video. As I compared and organized multiple parenting channels into Structure Maps, I gradually built my own standard for parenting.

three.quote: I was often confused because different channels made different claims about health, but comparing and organizing large amounts of health-related YouTube information into Structure Maps helped me build my own standard.

info.refund: • Credits are eligible for a full refund within 7 days of purchase if none of the credits charged by that order have been used.

faq.title: FAQ
faq.q2: How many credits does a video use?
faq.a2: Most content uses just 1 credit. Up to 70,000 characters uses 1 credit, 70,001 to 110,000 characters uses 2 credits, and anything over 110,000 characters can't be processed.
faq.a3: Paid credits are valid for 1 year from the payment date. Refund requests are available within 7 days of purchase, and only orders whose credited amount has not been used are eligible for a full refund.
faq.q4: Will I receive a payment receipt?
faq.a4: Yes. LemonSqueezy sends a receipt email after payment. If you can’t find it or need help with a billing issue, contact us and we’ll help you check the order details.
